{"id":"StableVSR-79s","title":"Create unified inference CLI","description":"Build stablevsr CLI with infer, backend-info, doctor subcommands behind backend abstraction.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:49:51Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:49:51Z"}

new_value: Implemented src/stablevsr/backends/ with base.py, registry.py, torch_backend.py, mlx_backend.py. Auto-detection works for mps/cuda/cpu.
Implemented CLI with backend-info and doctor subcommands. Entry point registered in pyproject.toml. All checks pass.
Phase 1 complete: pyproject.toml, package structure, dependency extras, documentation
Phase 2 complete: Backend abstraction with torch/mlx, registry, auto-detection, env var override
Phase 3 complete: CLI with backend-info, doctor; 16 passing tests
Phase 4 complete: MLX backend scaffold with honest capability reporting. Full inference via torch-mps.
Phase 6 initial: 16 tests (14 backend, 2 flow_utils), pytest configured in pyproject.toml

old_value: {"id":"StableVSR-gfd","title":"Fix CUDA hardcoding in pipeline/stablevsr_pipeline.py","description":"enable_model_cpu_offload() at L228 hardcodes torch.device(f'cuda:{gpu_id}') which crashes on MPS/CPU. torch.cuda.empty_cache() at L1066 is unguarded. Both are in the inference hot path and break Apple Silicon usage.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:48Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:48Z"}

{"id":"StableVSR-gfd","title":"Fix CUDA hardcoding in pipeline/stablevsr_pipeline.py","description":"enable_model_cpu_offload() at L228 hardcodes torch.device(f'cuda:{gpu_id}') which crashes on MPS/CPU. torch.cuda.empty_cache() at L1066 is unguarded. Both are in the inference hot path and break Apple Silicon usage.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"sealad886","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:48Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:08:57Z"}

new_value: Fixed both CUDA hardcodings in pipeline/stablevsr_pipeline.py: (1) enable_model_cpu_offload() now auto-detects CUDA/MPS/CPU device instead of hardcoding cuda:{gpu_id}, (2) torch.cuda.empty_cache() guarded behind torch.cuda.is_available(). No new errors introduced — all reported errors are pre-existing library stub issues.

old_value: {"id":"StableVSR-1as","title":"Fix pyproject.toml: platform markers and mlx-nn package name","description":"1) [train] extra includes bitsandbytes\u003e=0.43 which is CUDA-only and fails to install on macOS. Add platform marker: bitsandbytes\u003e=0.43; sys_platform != 'darwin'. 2) [mlx] extra lists mlx-nn\u003e=0.12 which may not exist as a standalone pip package. Verify and fix. 3) Remove xformers from train extra if present or add CUDA platform marker.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:48Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:48Z"}

{"id":"StableVSR-1as","title":"Fix pyproject.toml: platform markers and mlx-nn package name","description":"1) [train] extra includes bitsandbytes\u003e=0.43 which is CUDA-only and fails to install on macOS. Add platform marker: bitsandbytes\u003e=0.43; sys_platform != 'darwin'. 2) [mlx] extra lists mlx-nn\u003e=0.12 which may not exist as a standalone pip package. Verify and fix. 3) Remove xformers from train extra if present or add CUDA platform marker.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"sealad886","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:48Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:10:47Z"}

new_value: Fixed 3 issues: (1) bitsandbytes gets sys_platform != 'darwin' marker, (2) added xformers with same darwin guard for train extra, (3) removed non-existent mlx-nn package and added sys_platform == 'darwin' marker to mlx extra. All changes verified in pyproject.toml.

old_value: {"id":"StableVSR-hgz","title":"Implement 'stablevsr infer' CLI subcommand","description":"The modernization audit (Phase 3) promised a unified inference CLI subcommand but only backend-info and doctor were implemented. Add 'stablevsr infer' that accepts input frames/video, output path, model options, and uses the backend abstraction layer. Acceptance criteria: stablevsr infer --input \u003cpath\u003e --output \u003cpath\u003e works with torch backend on MPS/CPU.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z"}

{"id":"StableVSR-hgz","title":"Implement 'stablevsr infer' CLI subcommand","description":"The modernization audit (Phase 3) promised a unified inference CLI subcommand but only backend-info and doctor were implemented. Add 'stablevsr infer' that accepts input frames/video, output path, model options, and uses the backend abstraction layer. Acceptance criteria: stablevsr infer --input \u003cpath\u003e --output \u003cpath\u003e works with torch backend on MPS/CPU.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"sealad886","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:12:06Z"}

new_value: Implemented 'stablevsr infer' CLI subcommand. Accepts --input, --output, --model-id, --controlnet-ckpt, --steps, --seed, --backend. Uses backend abstraction for device selection. Handles both subdirectory and flat folder inputs. Makes xformers optional (CUDA only). No new real errors — only pre-existing library stub warnings.

old_value: {"id":"StableVSR-y6s","title":"Phase 5: Modernize test.py and eval.py device handling","description":"test.py L33 hardcodes torch.device('cuda'), L40 uses enable_xformers unconditionally. eval.py L32 hardcodes cuda, L38-40 hardcodes device='cuda' in pyiqa metrics. Fix: auto-detect device using backend abstraction or simple cuda/mps/cpu fallback. Make xformers optional. Fix hardcoded /home/crota/ paths in eval.py L22.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z"}

{"id":"StableVSR-y6s","title":"Phase 5: Modernize test.py and eval.py device handling","description":"test.py L33 hardcodes torch.device('cuda'), L40 uses enable_xformers unconditionally. eval.py L32 hardcodes cuda, L38-40 hardcodes device='cuda' in pyiqa metrics. Fix: auto-detect device using backend abstraction or simple cuda/mps/cpu fallback. Make xformers optional. Fix hardcoded /home/crota/ paths in eval.py L22.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"sealad886","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:15:24Z"}

new_value: Modernized device handling in test.py and eval.py: (1) test.py uses CUDA→MPS→CPU auto-detection, xformers guarded behind CUDA check, (2) eval.py uses auto-detection, pyiqa metrics use device variable instead of 'cuda' string, gt_path default changed from hardcoded /home/crota/ to None with required validation.

old_value: {"id":"StableVSR-rru","title":"Phase 5: Modernize train.py and train.sh device handling","description":"train.py has pervasive CUDA assumptions: xformers (L97-98), bitsandbytes 8-bit Adam, tf32 (L759), torch.autocast('cuda'). train.sh hardcodes CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES and --enable_xformers. Also dataset/config_reds.yaml has hardcoded /home/crota/ paths. Fix: make xformers/bitsandbytes optional with graceful fallbacks, use device-aware autocast, parameterize paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z"}

{"id":"StableVSR-rru","title":"Phase 5: Modernize train.py and train.sh device handling","description":"train.py has pervasive CUDA assumptions: xformers (L97-98), bitsandbytes 8-bit Adam, tf32 (L759), torch.autocast('cuda'). train.sh hardcodes CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES and --enable_xformers. Also dataset/config_reds.yaml has hardcoded /home/crota/ paths. Fix: make xformers/bitsandbytes optional with graceful fallbacks, use device-aware autocast, parameterize paths.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"sealad886","owner":"155285242+sealad886@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:07:49Z","created_by":"sealad886","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:16:43Z"}

new_value: Modernized train.py (xformers warns instead of crashing, tf32 guarded behind CUDA check, 8-bit Adam requires CUDA), train.sh (replaced /home/crota/ paths with configurable variables), dataset/config_reds.yaml (replaced hardcoded paths with /path/to/ placeholders, meta_info_file now relative).
